mission.title: container-network-interface-cni-: Fixed the check for network namespace path.
mission.description:
The expectation on older kernels (< 3.19) was to have the network
namespace always be a directory. This is not true if the network
namespace is bind mounted to a file, and will make the plugin fail
erroneously in such cases.

I have tried this with Mesos on a CentOS 7 cluster, running 3.10 kernel and it works fine. Moreover, have also tried it on Ubuntu 14.04 which has 3.13 kernel and works fine on that as well. 

This should fix #288

resolution.summary:
Fixed the namespace path check in `pkg/ns` package in the upstream `containernetworking/cni` repo:
https://github.com/containernetworking/cni/pull/290

Updating the CNI plugins package to reflect this PR.

Fixes https://dcosjira.atlassian.net/browse/DCOS-300
steps: Review the issue discussion for context
Apply the fix from the linked pull request
